content: The orthographic and lexical dual identity of "reflexion" (the variant spelling of the English word reflection meaning introspection, rooted in language education vocabulary) can act as a natural structural template for multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms: LLMs can be prompted to generate two outputs under the labels "reflexion" (the old spelling variant, representing a raw, unedited first attempt at problem-solving) and "reflection" (the standard spelling, representing deliberate critical re-evaluation of the first attempt).
